mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.4 percentage points over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of observed cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side growth that offsets rate pressure and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles yield negative 20-day returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate coinciding with a rising unemployment rate signals proactive central bank easing to counter labor market weakness, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate market repricing of inflation expectations and potential policy tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.0% quarterly GDP growth combined with slowing industrial production indicates economic stagnation that suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered cases across all tested samples.
